core_inference.name: audio_preprocessing
core_inference.description: Load audio/video, extract mono audio, resample, trim silence, and normalize level.
core_inference.generalization_note: General-purpose preprocessing that does not depend on a specific song pattern.

core_inference.name: frame_level_pitch_tracking
core_inference.description: Estimate frame-level F0 candidates and voiced/unvoiced states from the waveform.
core_inference.generalization_note: Core monophonic pitch extraction; intended to generalize across clean single-note inputs.

core_inference.name: onset_aware_note_segmentation
core_inference.description: Convert frame-level pitch into raw note events using onset evidence, pitch continuity, and minimum duration logic.
core_inference.generalization_note: General note-boundary inference for plucked single-note audio.

core_inference.name: beat_and_measure_inference
core_inference.description: Estimate BPM, beat grid, measure boundaries, and base quantization phase from audio timing evidence.
core_inference.generalization_note: General rhythmic inference layer, but still imperfect for complex subdivisions.

core_inference.name: pattern_level_rhythm_decoding
core_inference.description: Decode beat-local rhythm patterns such as quarter, eighth-pair, sixteenth groups, and triplets from quantized note timing.
core_inference.generalization_note: General symbolic rhythm interpretation, but sensitive to upstream segmentation quality.

heuristic_corrections.name: closed_set_pitch_constraint
heuristic_corrections.description: Restrict candidate pitches to a known note set when the caller provides candidate notes.
heuristic_corrections.generalization_note: Useful for benchmarked exercises, but not a general open-set recognition ability.

heuristic_corrections.name: same_pitch_fragment_consolidation
heuristic_corrections.description: Merge or preserve same-pitch neighboring fragments based on confidence, gap size, and repeated-run context.
heuristic_corrections.generalization_note: Generalizable heuristic for plucked repeated notes, but still threshold-driven.

heuristic_corrections.name: stable_anchor_alignment_after_tuplet_run
heuristic_corrections.description: After a same-pitch tuplet run, prefer aligning a following different-pitch stable anchor to the next strong beat when the raw timing supports that interpretation.
heuristic_corrections.generalization_note: Abstracted from case-specific failures, but intended as a general rule for repeated-run to anchor transitions.

heuristic_corrections.name: anchor_sequence_alignment_after_repeated_tuplets
heuristic_corrections.description: When repeated same-pitch tuplets are followed by a sequence of long anchors with consistent raw spacing, promote those anchors to consecutive integer beats.
heuristic_corrections.generalization_note: Generalized from repeated-run benchmark failures; still a heuristic correction rather than pure model inference.

heuristic_corrections.name: repeated_note_rhythm_regularization
heuristic_corrections.description: Regularize dense same-pitch note runs toward a more consistent symbolic rhythm when raw timing strongly suggests a repeated pattern.
heuristic_corrections.generalization_note: General post-processing heuristic that can help or overfit depending on the sample.

validation_observations.notes: Current rules were actively tuned against case3 to fix triplet-to-anchor transitions. This improves benchmark fit but does not by itself prove generalization.
No matching unseen local sample with a similar triplet-plus-anchor structure has been validated yet.
Case2 is rhythmically different from case3. It is still useful as a regression check to ensure new heuristics do not break non-triplet repeated-note material.
